message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the deep 328-basket render RELIABLY COMPLETE — no deadlock, no degrade-to-seed — keeping full coverage + faithfulness + agentic. Per the completed speed investigation (/home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md FINAL SYNTHESIS): the clean run is already ~24min (near floor); there is NO big legal speedup on the clean path. The two REAL wins are P0 (ship the safe non-deadlocking compose config so the 16-way HANG becomes a clean completion) and P1 (fix the degrade-to-seed bad tail). DO NOT re-attempt the disqualified levers (parallelize react-loop turns — blocked by the serial decide dependency; fetch cache; more compose/section concurrency; turn caps; basket-worker sharding at scale — each deadlocks or drops coverage or drifts verdicts).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "STEP 0: reviewed the 2 uncommitted files — kept the WHEEL_PROGRESS deadlock note; kept scripts/_run_16way_s3gear329.sh but re-headed it as the guard-REFUSED A/B certification harness for the deadlock config.",
    "STEP 1 (P0): wrote src/polaris_graph/generator/compose_config_guard.py — assert_safe_compose_config() REFUSES PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY>=48 unless PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED=1 attests a full-328 verdict-identity A/B passed; wired it at the top of generate_multi_section_report (fires before any work). Did NOT add a timeout to entailment_judge acquire (would fail-closed->drop sentences->faithfulness breach) and did NOT ship the judge-cap clamp, per mission.",
    "Pinned the confirmed-safe config in scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py (BASKET_WORKERS=1, side-judge=8, PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3; off-loop already shipped). Surfaced outline_agent_stats (cp4_used) on MultiSectionResult + compose_summary.json so the full render can prove agentic.",
    "STEP 2 (P1): outline_agent.py grace default 180->600s (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS) so a legit final in-flight turn containing a ~466s mega-fetch COMPLETES agentic instead of being cancelled by the outer asyncio.wait_for -> degrade-to-seed. No fetch-cap, no turn cut.",
    "STEP 3: ran the FULL 328-basket deep render end-to-end on data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json under the safe config; monitored thread/socket/IO state live to distinguish healthy network-bound work from the futex wedge; it completed clean."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "5168fe8",
    "f484b57",
    "adfe4e4",
    "56d7ed0"
  ],
  "files": [
    "src/polaris_graph/generator/compose_config_guard.py",
    "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py",
    "src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py",
    "scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py",
    "scripts/_run_16way_s3gear329.sh",
    "tests/test_compose_config_guard.py",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "docs/step3_full328_render/compose_summary.json"
  ],
  "exercised": "Guard: `pytest tests/test_compose_config_guard.py` 15/15 pass + a live call to generate_multi_section_report with PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=16/PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=48 raised UnsafeComposeConfigError before any compute. Degrade fix: a standalone asyncio reproduction of the exact outline_agent.py:2054 wait_for+degrade structure showed cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed' under grace=180 and 'agentic' under grace=600 for a run overshooting the wall by 300s and by the documented 466s. Full render: a real 43.2min end-to-end 328-basket run on the corrected corpus with the safe config, verified clean via log + process thread/socket/IO inspection.",
  "measurement": "FULL 328-basket agentic deep render, real end-to-end run (outputs/step3_full328_render, exit 0): WALL=2589.7s (43.2min). NO deadlock, NO SIGKILL/OOM (0 killed/oom/deadlock/traceback in log; main thread stayed in ep_poll with IO byte-counters rising throughout — +22KB write/+10KB read per 30s sample). cp4_used=agentic (degraded_to_seed=False, turns=1, degrade_reason=''). Faithfulness PASS: leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved_markers=[]. baskets=329 (>=328), 11 sections kept / 0 dropped, 2394 verified words, 60 bib entries. Guard: 15/15 pytest + proven refusal at the real generate_multi_section_report entry point (workers=16/sem=48 -> UnsafeComposeConfigError before any work). Degrade fix: behavioral asyncio proof (run overshooting wall by 300s/466s degrades under grace=180, completes agentic under 600).",
  "open": [
    "WALL is 43.2min, NOT ~24min: the deep route-all render composes all 328 baskets SERIALLY under the safe config (BASKET_WORKERS=1). The 16-way config that would cut this to ~24min DEADLOCKS and is now guard-refused. So reliable-completion currently costs ~1.8x wall vs the (unsafe) 16-way.",
    "The P1 degrade-tail fix was proven at unit/behavioral level only — this pre-built corpus never tripped the 900s outline wall (outline finished at turn 1, no mega-fetch), so the real run did not exercise the grace change. A live-retrieval corpus with a mega-fetch would be needed to stress it end-to-end.",
    "moat_quantified_models=0 (the agent did not invoke verified_compute on this corpus) and strict_verify dropped 112/192 sentences (corpus-density span limit) — unchanged from prior rounds, not regressions."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS"
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The safe config makes the 328-basket render RELIABLY COMPLETE (proven: no deadlock, agentic, faithfulness PASS) but at 43.2min vs the ~24min mission target, because it composes all 328 route-all baskets serially (BASKET_WORKERS=1). The only lever that recovers ~24min is intra-section basket concurrency, which is exactly what deadlocked at 16-way. Decision: should I invest next round in certifying a BOUNDED middle ground (e.g. BASKET_WORKERS=2-4 with a bounded, timeout-guarded side-judge acquire) via a real full-328 verdict-identity A/B to buy back wall-time under the guard's escape hatch — or is reliable-43min the correct answer and we should NOT chase 24min at any deadlock risk (i.e. accept the wall and treat 24min as unachievable on the safe path)?



DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify Opus's claims against the real artifacts before deciding. First, the speed investigation's ~24min claim vs the 43.2min run, and the actual diffs.

input.description: Read final synthesis of speed investigation

content.content:
topic-judge). Zero-yield stretch 179s (24%).
      C  dedup/consolidate/cred      33s   2.3%
      D  COMPOSE + strict_verify    514s  35.5%   (65 verified / 68 dropped)
      E  fact_dedup/render           27s   1.9%
    The brief's "30+ min / ~24-min retrieval" is the BAD-TAIL case (_16way_run: one
    466s mega-fetch of 162/200 URLs, then wall-clock TimeoutError -> DEGRADE TO SEED).
    SINGLE BIGGEST HONEST LEVER: there is no surviving legal optimization that dents
    the 737s clean-run loop. The biggest honest lever is TAIL CONTROL — the ~6-10 min
    gap between the 24.2-min clean run and the 30+-min bad-tail run, which is also an
    I3 QUALITY bug (that tail run degraded to seed). No mechanism for it has yet been
    adversarially cleared; it is the top candidate for the next investigation round.

(2) RANKED PLAN (most wall-time protected/saved first, all 4 invariants kept):
  P0 — PIN THE SAFE COMPOSE CONFIG (protects completion; saving vs live baseline = 0;
       vs the 16-way attempt = hang -> completion).
       Do: keep the shipped off-loop wrap (multi_section_generator.py:6079/:6130
       asyncio.to_thread) with PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS unset/1 (default, confirmed:
       verified_compose.py _compose_basket_workers coerces <=1 to 1; no override in
       /workspace/POLARIS/.env or launch scripts). Keep PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY
       in the 4-8/host band; PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3.
       GUARD: add a startup assert that refuses/clamps PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1
       (and any sem>=48 judge cap) until the re-engineered path in (3) exists and has
       passed a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B. Never re-run the sem(48) config.
  P1 — FIX THE BAD TAIL AS A CORRECTNESS BUG (worth ~6-10 min on affected runs, and
       required for I3 regardless of speed). The 466s mega-fetch + 900s wall trip is
       what produces both the 30+-min runs AND degrade-to-seed. No cleared mechanism
       yet. Constraint for any future fix: must not cut fetch_cap/coverage and must
       not cap turns; raising the outline wall for route_all deep runs is quality-
       preserving but SLOWER — acceptable, because a degraded run is worth zero.
  P2 — 100%-OVERLAP PER-ASPECT EXHAUSTION SKIP (the only salvageable affirmative
       lever; DEFERRED to next wheel — needs the live_retriever/outline seam, which is
       read-only while the wheel is live). Skip the fetch+classify slice iff EVERY
       candidate URL (after the SAME url-dedup rule, never query text) is already in
       ev_store — then kept=0 is guaranteed, ev_store is byte-identical, checklist
       inputs identical, hence verdict-identical by construction. Keep gap-ledger
       transitions (mark_retry_or_unfilled) unchanged. NOTE: this is the legal subset
       of R2; the 90% variant stays disqualified (I1/I2).
       Measured ceiling (clean run): turns 8-11 fetch stamps 17.0+22.3+16.9+18.0 =
       74s + fold/classify overhead inside the 179s zero-yield stretch => ~1-2 min,
       NOT the earlier ~2-3 min claim (decide/checklist in that stretch must stay).
       GATE: full-328 verdict-identity A/B + cp4_used==agentic + ~328/329 rendered.
  P3 — NOTHING ELSE. Disqualified (do not re-propose without new evidence): R1 batch
       gap drain (checklist IS the convergence/discovery engine; batching changes the
       query set -> I2); R3 cheaper decide (routing selects evidence -> I2; decides
       total only ~170s); turn/wall caps (I1/I3); basket sharding incl. the judge-cap
       clamp (collapses to serial at safe knobs; concurrency requires J~48 -> 429
       storm -> I2; I4 guards unimplementable as specified); judge-cap moves in either
       direction (correctness knob); batched judge prompts (I2 by construction);
       bytes fetch-cache (0 on a cold single run, not wired); section concurrency
       3->8 (bounded by global LLM sem=5 -> tens of seconds); draft-prompt tightening
       / regen skip (I2; regen is the coverage-rescue path); intra-search fetch
       concurrency (already exists — the one CONFIRMED finding).

(3) COMPOSE DEADLOCK — EXACT SAFE FIX:
  Root cause (code-verified, static): multiplicative thread oversubscription with no
  escape valve — PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS(3) x PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS(16) map threads,
  each NLI POST spawning an inner ThreadPoolExecutor(1) (~2x amplification), all
  contending on ONE process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore; the BINDING entailment
  acquire has NO timeout (entailment_judge.py:155); the basket-map join and the outer
  asyncio.to_thread have no wall; `with ThreadPoolExecutor` __exit__ blocks on a
  wedged worker (shutdown(wait=True)); to_thread is non-cancellable. Raising the sem
  to 48 moved the wedge and stripped the 429 de-storm; it is not a fix.
  THE FIX THAT SHIPS: off-loop + workers=1, enforced by the P0 startup guard. Do NOT
  add a timeout to the binding entailment acquire — JudgeSlotTimeout emits the fail-
  closed ('ENTAILED','judge_error') sentinel that consumers DROP, changing the
  kept/dropped set (I2). Do NOT ship the judge-cap clamp (disqualified D1-D6: yields
  workers=1 at safe knobs anyway; ignores inner verify width V; misdiagnoses the
  wedge). workers>1 stays OFF the table until a re-engineered path exists (dedicated
  module-owned executor off the to_thread default pool + complete demand model incl.
  V + a watchdog that can actually free a wedge) AND passes a full-328 A/B.
  OFF-LOOP-ONLY FALLBACK — CONFIRMED deadlock-free AND verdict-identical:
    - workers=1 => _parallel_baskets False => NO nested basket pool; serial MAP+REDUCE
      in original basket order = byte-identical => verdict-identical by construction.
    - Peak judge-slot demand = PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS(3) <= cap(4); each POST bounded by
      the 150s total-deadline; no re-entrancy (verify loop is serial, holds <=1 slot).
    - EMPIRICAL: the full 329-basket route_all COMPLETED clean on exactly this config
      (1449.7s, no SIGKILL, faithfulness_pass=true, leaked_cite_ev_tokens=0,
      verified=65/dropped=68 gate intact, finish_outline bounced at turn 18 and kept
      looping — agentic held, no degrade line).

(4) HONEST CEILING:
  - A faithful, agentic, full-coverage 328/329-basket deep render runs ~24 min TODAY
    when nothing goes wrong (1449.7s measured, all four invariants verified in-log).
    That is already close to the floor.
  - After P2 lands (next wheel, A/B-gated): ~22-23 min clean. After P1: the 30+-min
    tail collapses toward the clean number on runs that would have gone bad.
  - Realistic floor ~20-22 min on current models. CANNOT be sped up without
    sacrificing quality:
      * The serial react-loop LLM chain (~391s decide+checklist): each query is chosen
        from the previous fold — it IS the agentic convergence engine; parallelizing,
        batching, or cheapening it changes the evidence pool => verdict drift (I2).
      * Cross-turn fetch overlap: blocked by the same decide dependency; intra-search
        fetch is already parallel and bounded by the slowest URL.
      * strict_verify/NLI: per-sentence entailment at unstarved token budgets under
        the 4-8 judge cap — the faithfulness gate itself; every "verify faster" idea
        examined (batching, timeouts, cap raises) changes verdicts.
      * Drop-driven repair/regen (~130-180s of compose): regen is the coverage-rescue
        path; the only way to shrink it changes draft text => changes verdicts.
      * Coverage itself: 329 baskets must each be composed and verified; that work is
        the product, not overhead.

commit 5168fe8e70a5cd0b00426d0f46770f9005e3d0c9
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 06:04:49 2026 +0000

    P0 STEP1: startup guard refuses deadlocking compose config + pin confirmed-safe config
    
    The FULL 328-basket 16-way compose DEADLOCKED (19/20 threads futex_wait, SIGKILL). Add
    compose_config_guard.assert_safe_compose_config(): refuses PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or
    PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY>=48 unless PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED=1 attests a
    full-328 verdict-identity A/B passed. Wired at the top of generate_multi_section_report (fires
    before any work; proven at the real entry point). Pin the confirmed-safe config in the launch
    driver (workers=1, side-judge=8, parallel_sections=3). Surface outline_agent_stats (cp4_used) on
    MultiSectionResult so the full render PROVES agentic (not degraded-seed). 16way harness re-headed
    as the guard-refused A/B certification tool. 15/15 guard regression tests pass.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 scripts/_run_16way_s3gear329.sh                    |  30 ++++++
 scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py        |  24 +++++

commit f484b575eb4d54902cb0339c446bb0babe88e175
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 06:05:02 2026 +0000

    P1 STEP2: park the outer outline wall so a legit mega-fetch completes agentic (no degrade-to-seed)
    
    Degrade tail: the internal loop stops STARTING turns at wall(900s) but a turn already in flight
    runs to completion; a single legit search_more_evidence mega-fetch (observed 162/200 URLs in
    466.2s, bounded only by the retrieval deadline checked BETWEEN per-query fetches) pushed the final
    in-flight turn past wall+180s grace -> outer asyncio.wait_for CANCELLED it -> TimeoutError ->
    cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed'. Raise the grace default 180 -> 600 (env PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_
    TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS) so the final turn COMPLETES (quality-preserving, NO fetch-cap, NO turn cut,
    zero coverage loss) then the loop returns normally as agentic. Still a finite ceiling (wall+grace
    ~25min) that catches a TRUE hang, since every inner call is itself bounded. Behavioral proof:
    run overshooting wall by 300s/466s degrades under 180 but completes agentic under 600.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py | 15 ++++++++++++++-
 1 file changed, 14 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

1	"""P0 DEADLOCK GUARD (2026-07-12) — refuse the known-deadlocking compose config at STARTUP.
2	
3	FORENSIC ROOT CAUSE (this wheel's mission; speed_investigation.md FINAL SYNTHESIS): a FULL
4	328-basket 16-way agentic compose DEADLOCKED — 19/20 threads wedged in ``futex_wait``, 0 progress
5	for 8.8 min, SIGKILLed. The cause is MULTIPLICATIVE thread oversubscription: ``PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS``
6	(3) x ``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS`` (16) x the ~2 inner ThreadPoolExecutors all contend on ONE
7	process-global side-judge ``BoundedSemaphore``; the binding entailment ``acquire`` has NO timeout and
8	``with ThreadPoolExecutor`` ``shutdown(wait=True)`` re-hangs on an already-wedged worker. The small
9	verdict-identity A/B PASSED but did NOT exercise the full-scale semaphore + basket-worker + off-loop
10	interaction, so the deadlock only appeared at 328-basket scale.
11	
12	THE GUARD (LAW VI, env-driven; NEVER silently mutates config): the two config dimensions that push the
13	compose into the un-certified deadlock regime are
14	
15	  * ``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS`` > 1  — intra-section basket map concurrency, and
16	  * ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` >= 48 — the global side-judge in-flight ceiling.
17	
18	Either one, at 328-basket scale, is UN-CERTIFIED: no FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has proven it both
19	completes (no hang) AND stays verdict-identical. So ``assert_safe_compose_config()`` REFUSES to start a
20	compose in that regime by raising ``UnsafeComposeConfigError`` — UNLESS the operator sets the explicit
21	certification flag ``PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED=1``, which is the CONTRACT that a full-328
22	verdict-identity A/B has actually been run and passed. The deadlock can therefore never ship again by
23	accident: shipping it now requires a human to assert, via that env flag, that the A/B was done.
24	
25	The CONFIRMED-SAFE config (the clean 24.2 min / 1449.7 s run used it): off-loop ON,
26	``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS``=1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE), ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` in
27	the 4-8 band, ``PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS``=3 — faithfulness PASS, agentic held, no hang. That config sails
28	through this guard untouched (workers==1, sem<48). The guard changes NOTHING about a safe run; it only
29	fails-fast a KNOWN-BAD one before it can wedge the box.
30	
31	Stdlib-only leaf module -> zero import cost on the safe path.
32	"""
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import os
36	
37	# The un-certified deadlock thresholds (see module docstring for the forensic derivation).
38	DEADLOCK_BASKET_WORKERS_MIN = 2        # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS > 1 (i.e. >= 2) is un-certified.
39	DEADLOCK_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM_MIN = 48       # PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY >= 48 is un-certified.
40	
41	ENV_BASKET_WORKERS = "PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS"
42	ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM = "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY"
43	# The ONE escape hatch: an operator sets this to attest that a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B was run
44	# AND passed (both no-hang AND verdict-identical). Without it the deadlock config is refused.
45	ENV_AB_CERTIFIED = "PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED"
46	
47	_TRUE = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
48	
49	
50	class UnsafeComposeConfigError(RuntimeError):
51	    """Raised at compose startup when a KNOWN-DEADLOCKING config is requested without A/B certification.
52	
53	    Fail-CLOSED by design: a compose must never begin in the multiplicative-oversubscription regime
54	    that wedged the box, unless a human has explicitly attested (via ``PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_
55	    AB_CERTIFIED=1``) that a full-328 verdict-identity A/B certified it both completes and stays
56	    verdict-identical."""
57	
58	
59	def _int_env(env: "dict[str, str] | os._Environ[str]", name: str, default: int) -> int:
60	    """Read ``name`` as an int from ``env``; on unset/blank/malformed return ``default`` (fail to the
61	    SAFE reading — a typo must never silently DISABLE the guard by parsing as a huge/zero value)."""
62	    raw = str(env.get(name, "")).strip()
63	    if raw == "":
64	        return default
65	    try:
66	        return int(raw)
67	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
68	        return default
69	
70	
71	def evaluate_compose_config(env=None) -> "tuple[bool, list[str], bool]":
72	    """Pure evaluation (no raise) of the compose config safety.
73	
74	    Returns ``(is_safe, violations, certified)`` where ``violations`` names each un-certified
75	    deadlock dimension that is active. ``is_safe`` is True iff there are NO violations OR the operator
76	    set the A/B certification flag. Pure/side-effect-free so tests and telemetry can call it freely."""
77	    env = os.environ if env is None else env
78	    certified = str(env.get(ENV_AB_CERTIFIED, "")).strip().lower() in _TRUE
79	    # Basket workers default 1 (serial); side-judge sem default 4 (judge_concurrency.DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY).
80	    workers = _int_env(env, ENV_BASKET_WORKERS, 1)
81	    sem = _int_env(env, ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM, 4)
82	    violations: list[str] = []
83	    if workers >= DEADLOCK_BASKET_WORKERS_MIN:
84	        violations.append(
85	            f"{ENV_BASKET_WORKERS}={workers} (>1: intra-section basket concurrency — un-certified "
86	            f"at 328-basket scale; DEADLOCKED 19/20 threads in futex_wait)"
87	        )
88	    if sem >= DEADLOCK_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM_MIN:
89	        violations.append(
90	            f"{ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM}={sem} (>={DEADLOCK_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM_MIN}: global side-judge "
91	            f"in-flight ceiling — un-certified; the binding entailment acquire has NO timeout)"
92	        )
93	    is_safe = (not violations) or certified
94	    return is_safe, violations, certified
95	
96	
97	def assert_safe_compose_config(env=None) -> None:
98	    """STARTUP GUARD: refuse a known-deadlocking compose config unless A/B-certified.
99	
100	    Call ONCE at the top of every compose entry point. Raises ``UnsafeComposeConfigError`` iff a
101	    deadlock dimension is active (``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS``>1 or ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY``
102	    >=48) AND ``PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED`` is not set. A SAFE config (the default, and
103	    the confirmed 24.2 min clean run) is a no-op. When certified, it does NOT raise but the caller may
104	    log the attestation."""
105	    is_safe, violations, certified = evaluate_compose_config(env)
106	    if is_safe:
107	        return
108	    raise UnsafeComposeConfigError(
109	        "REFUSING to start compose in a KNOWN-DEADLOCKING config (this wheel's mission: the FULL "
110	        "328-basket 16-way compose wedged 19/20 threads in futex_wait and was SIGKILLed). "
111	        "Un-certified deadlock dimension(s): "
112	        + "; ".join(violations)
113	        + f". To ship this config you MUST first run a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B proving it "
114	        f"BOTH completes (no hang) AND stays verdict-identical, then set {ENV_AB_CERTIFIED}=1 to "
115	        "attest it. The CONFIRMED-SAFE config is PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 + "
116	        "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY in 4-8 + PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3 (off-loop ON)."
117	    )
118

content.text: The guard is sound. Now the grace change and the run artifacts — especially the timing discrepancy vs the 1449.7s baseline.

content.content:
commit f484b575eb4d54902cb0339c446bb0babe88e175
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 06:05:02 2026 +0000

    P1 STEP2: park the outer outline wall so a legit mega-fetch completes agentic (no degrade-to-seed)
    
    Degrade tail: the internal loop stops STARTING turns at wall(900s) but a turn already in flight
    runs to completion; a single legit search_more_evidence mega-fetch (observed 162/200 URLs in
    466.2s, bounded only by the retrieval deadline checked BETWEEN per-query fetches) pushed the final
    in-flight turn past wall+180s grace -> outer asyncio.wait_for CANCELLED it -> TimeoutError ->
    cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed'. Raise the grace default 180 -> 600 (env PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_
    TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS) so the final turn COMPLETES (quality-preserving, NO fetch-cap, NO turn cut,
    zero coverage loss) then the loop returns normally as agentic. Still a finite ceiling (wall+grace
    ~25min) that catches a TRUE hang, since every inner call is itself bounded. Behavioral proof:
    run overshooting wall by 300s/466s degrades under 180 but completes agentic under 600.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py b/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py
index 01f40eb..4689809 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py
@@ -137,7 +137,20 @@ PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT = 131072
 # that hangs past its own inner timeout). Never the primary bound — ``_wall_seconds()``
 # is — this only guarantees the outer ``run_outline_agent_or_legacy`` caller is never
 # wedged indefinitely.
-PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 180
+# P1 DEGRADE-TAIL FIX (2026-07-12, this wheel): the internal loop stops STARTING new turns
+# at ``_wall_seconds()`` (900s) but a turn ALREADY in flight runs to completion — and a single
+# legitimate ``search_more_evidence`` mega-fetch (observed: 162/200 URLs in 466.2s, bounded only
+# by the retrieval deadline which is checked BETWEEN per-query fetches, not mid-batch) can push
+# that final in-flight turn well past the wall. With the old 180s grace the outer
+# ``asyncio.wait_for`` CANCELLED that legitimately-progressing final turn -> TimeoutError ->
+# DEGRADE-TO-SEED (cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed'), throwing away a good agentic run over a
+# slow-but-honest fetch. The mission fix is to PARK the wall: give the final in-flight turn enough
+# grace to COMPLETE (quality-preserving, even if slower — NO fetch-cap, NO turn cut, zero coverage
+# loss), after which the loop sees elapsed>=wall and returns NORMALLY as agentic. Raised 180 -> 600
+# to comfortably cover the documented ~466s mega-fetch overshoot with margin; still an ABSOLUTE
+# ceiling (wall+grace = ~25min) that catches a TRUE hang, because every inner call is itself bounded
+# (entailment 150s/call, retrieval deadline, decide/checklist max_tokens). Env-overridable (LAW VI).
+PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 600
 
 
 def outliner_agent_model() -> str:

1	{
2	  "corpus": "cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json",
3	  "judged_drb_task": "72",
4	  "composed_to_rq": "Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth",
5	  "corpus_rq": "I am researching the impact of Generative AI on the future labor market, please help me complete a research report. The report needs to be based on academic res",
6	  "report_title": "A literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market",
7	  "section_headings": [
8	    "Overview of AI-Induced Labor Market Restructuring",
9	    "Task-Based Models of Displacement and Reinstatement",
10	    "Occupational Exposure and AI Susceptibility",
11	    "Employment Displacement and Creation: Early Empirical Evidence",
12	    "Productivity Gains and Augmentation Effects of AI",
13	    "Wage Polarization and Distributional Consequences",
14	    "Skill Demands, Job Quality, and Workforce Transformation",
15	    "Policy Responses to AI-Driven Labor Restructuring",
16	    "Cross-Study Synthesis: Convergences and Contradictions",
17	    "Conclusions and Future Research Gaps",
18	    "Additional Corroborated Findings"
19	  ],
20	  "evidence_rows": 997,
21	  "baskets": 329,
22	  "same_work_groups": 55,
23	  "outline_sections": 11,
24	  "kept_sections": 11,
25	  "dropped_sections": 0,
26	  "total_words": 2394,
27	  "total_sentences_verified": 80,
28	  "total_sentences_dropped": 112,
29	  "bibliography_entries": 60,
30	  "report_chars": 27666,
31	  "report_words": 3467,
32	  "faithfulness_audit": {
33	    "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": 0,
34	    "leaked_cite_samples": [],
35	    "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": 54,
36	    "bibliography_entries": 60,
37	    "unresolved_markers": []
38	  },
39	  "faithfulness_pass": true,

41	  "degraded_to_seed": false,
42	  "degrade_reason": "",

input.gap_to_top: Wall is 2589.7s (43.2min) vs the ~24min (1449.7s) target — and the 1449.7s benchmark was measured on the SAME safe config (workers=1, off-loop, sections=3, full 329-basket route_all, per speed_investigation.md §3 and Opus's own guard docstring compose_config_guard.py:25). Worse: step3 had turns=1 (no ~737s agentic loop the baseline paid), so its compose phase took ~2500s vs the baseline's ~514-713s — ~3.5x slower for only ~1.44x the strict-verify load (192 vs 133 sentences). That +19min is UNEXPLAINED, not an inherent cost of serial compose. Secondary gap: the P1 degrade-tail fix (grace 180->600) was proven only via a standalone asyncio reproduction; the real run never tripped the 900s wall (turns=1), so criterion (3)'s 'real run' proof is outstanding. Criteria (2) guard: fully met (15/15 tests + live refusal at generate_multi_section_report entry).
input.next_step: Do NOT pursue the workers=2-4 A/B (the 'timeout-guarded side-judge acquire' is the disqualified JudgeSlotTimeout fail-closed sentinel — drops sentences, I2/faithfulness breach; and sharding collapses to serial at safe judge caps per speed_investigation P3). Instead, explain the +1140s compose anomaly on the safe path: re-run the step3 compose with PG_COMPOSE_TIMING=1 (the instrument Opus already landed in commit 3ff288c, multi_section_generator.py — default-off, byte-identical) and diff per-section/per-basket compose wall + strict_verify wall against the 1449.7s baseline's D-phase (~514s). Attribute the delta to (a) corpus density of cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (997 ev rows, 192 verify sentences — if so, restate the honest target for THIS corpus with numbers) vs (b) a fixable serial hotspot (side-judge sem=8 429-backoff vs baseline 4, regen retries, or a regression from the surfaced-stats/off-loop changes). Only after that breakdown decide if ~24min needs any concurrency at all — the evidence says it does not.
input.reason: Diffs verified real and correct: guard refuses workers>1 or sem>=48 without the A/B attestation flag, fail-safe int parsing, wired before any work, 15/15 tests plus live entry-point refusal; grace change is a single env-overridable default (180->600) that keeps a finite ~25min ceiling and adds no fail-closed drop path; run artifacts (compose_summary.json) confirm agentic, 329 baskets, leaked_cite_ev_tokens=0, unresolved_markers=[], no deadlock/SIGKILL. Faithfulness invariants intact — nothing touches the verify/fold seam, and the P0 decision to NOT add an entailment-acquire timeout preserved the no-dropped-sentence invariant. But the measurement is not top: 43.2min vs the ~24min target that was empirically achieved on the same safe config, and the P1 fix lacks a real-run proof. Opus's question poses a false binary; the mandatory next move is to profile the unexplained 1.8x compose slowdown on the already-safe path.
